spec.title: Active connections — nginx1
spec.description: Live connections on the master. Should hold all traffic when nginx2 is standby.

spec.title: NGINX connection pressure — per node
spec.description: Reading+writing as % of total active connections. Low = mostly idle keepalive slots. Climbing toward 100% = NGINX is saturated and actively processing most connections.

spec.title: Products API — response time percentiles
spec.description: p50/p95/p99 from the histogram. p99 climbing while p50 stays flat = occasional slow requests, not systemic.

spec.title: Auth API — response time percentiles
spec.description: Auth p95/p99. JWT signing/verification should be sub-millisecond — anything over 10ms warrants investigation.

spec.title: JWT tokens issued vs verified (rate/s)
spec.description: Issue rate vs verify rate. Large verify/issue ratio = many clients reusing tokens (expected). Verify rate dropping to zero = auth is broken.

spec.title: WebSocket — new connections + price updates (rate/s)
spec.description: products_ws_connections_total is a counter so rate() gives new connections/s. Price updates/s shows the feed is alive.

spec.title: Node.js event loop lag — products
spec.description: Event loop lag p50/p99. Values above ~50ms mean the JS thread is blocked. Correlate with high CPU or GC activity.

spec.title: Node.js event loop lag — auth
spec.description: Auth service event loop health. Should be consistently sub-millisecond since auth is CPU-light.

spec.title: CPU usage per VM
spec.description: Current CPU across all nodes. NGINX nodes should be near zero at idle.

spec.title: Memory usage per VM
spec.description: RAM utilisation. Node.js services typically stabilise around 50–100MB RSS.

spec.title: Network throughput — NGINX nodes
spec.description: Bytes in/out on private interface. The active master carries all traffic; standby near zero.

spec.title: Active connections — nginx2
spec.description: Should be near zero when nginx1 is master. Spikes here = failover in progress.

spec.title: Node.js heap used — both services
spec.description: Heap growth over time. Steady climb without GC dips = memory leak.

spec.title: Products service uptime
spec.description: Seconds since last restart. Sudden drop = crash or redeploy.

spec.title: Auth service uptime
spec.description: Seconds since last restart.

spec.title: Open file descriptors — both services
spec.description: FD count creeping toward process_max_fds = FD leak. Max is 1048576 on your nodes.

spec.title: Products created (cumulative)
spec.description: Total products created since service start. Flat line = no write traffic hitting the service.

spec.title: Cache hit ratio (current)
spec.description: Percentage of requests served from cache right now. Climb toward 100% as cache warms up. Drop = cache invalidated or TTL expired.

spec.title: Cache hit ratio over time
spec.description: Watch this climb as the cache warms up after a restart. Drop during the stale-while-revalidate demo.

spec.title: Cache events — HIT / MISS / BYPASS / STALE (rate/s)
spec.description: Raw cache event rates per second. MISS spikes on cold start or TTL expiry. STALE ticks up when backend is down and proxy_cache_use_stale serves saved responses. BYPASS should flatline.

spec.title: Stale responses served (total)
spec.description: Cumulative stale responses since last awk run. Non-zero during your demo = proxy_cache_use_stale is working correctly.

spec.title: Keepalive waiting (both nodes)
spec.description: Idle keepalive slots — high value means clients are reusing connections well.

spec.title: Reading + writing (both nodes)
spec.description: Connections actively processing a request right now.

spec.title: Active connections over time — per node
spec.description: The key failover panel. Kill nginx1 and watch its line drop to zero while nginx2 spikes. Record this for your demo.

spec.title: Request rate (req/s) — per node
spec.description: Confirms traffic is flowing through the VIP holder only.

spec.title: Connections accepted vs handled
spec.description: These should overlap. Any gap = NGINX dropping connections under load.

spec.title: Dropped connections/s
spec.description: Accepted minus handled. Must be zero in normal operation — any non-zero value needs investigation.

spec.title: Error rate % over time — per service
spec.description: 4xx+5xx as % of total. auth uses status_code label; products uses status label.
